content.text:
| MCP:Yl3et9 | # Fastapi Project Template
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | A FastAPI project template using Make, Docker Compose and Github Actions,
| MCP:Yl3et9 | that includes **SQLAlchemy**, **Alembic** and an integration test setup.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | [![Code Integration](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml)
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ##  Installation
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | The only requirements to develop are **Docker** and **Make**.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Docker makes it easy to get started and enables easier switching between projects, operating systems and machines.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | To start up the project locally you first clone the project, and then run the following command in the cloned directory:
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```sh
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ git clone https://github.com/Donnype/fastapi-template.git
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ cd fastapi-template
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ make up
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Then to update the schema using alembic, in another shell:
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```sh
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ make migrate
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```
| MCP:Yl3et9 | **That's it**. The app is set up and should be running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Code changes are automatically detected using a mounted docker volume.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ### Makefile
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ---
| MCP:Yl3et9 | The Makefile is the 'entrypoint' for the tools in this structure,
| MCP:Yl3et9 | such that you can easily run different commands without remembering the exact arguments.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Run `make help` to get an overview of the available commands:
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```sh
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ make help
| MCP:Yl3et9 | up:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Run the application
| MCP:Yl3et9 | done: check test
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Prepare for a commit
| MCP:Yl3et9 | test: utest itest
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:Yl3et9 | utest: cleantest
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Run unit tests
| MCP:Yl3et9 | itest: cleantest
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Run integration tests
| MCP:Yl3et9 | check: cleantest
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Check the code base
| MCP:Yl3et9 | cleantest:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Clean up test containers
| MCP:Yl3et9 | migrations:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:Yl3et9 | migrate:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:Yl3et9 | downgrade:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:Yl3et9 | help:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |  Display this help message
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Make allows you to collect common scripts and commands for the project.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Other projects using different programming languages and frameworks get the same development interface by using Make.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ---
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ### Installation without Docker
| MCP:Yl3et9 | To install a local copy of the python environment (to get code-completion for example),
| MCP:Yl3et9 | you must have **Poetry** installed.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Create a poetry environment by running `poetry install` at the root of the project.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | To start up the server in the poetry environment and talk to a test sqlite database in `./test.db`, run
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```bash
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```
| MCP:Yl3et9 | Again, the app should be all set up and running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ### Tests
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ---
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | The tests are containerized and the Docker setup can be found in the `.ci/` folder.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | They are written using Pytest.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | You can run the tests using:
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```bash
| MCP:Yl3et9 | $ make test
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ```
| MCP:Yl3et9 | This runs the integration & unit tests.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | If you want to run them separately, use `make itest` to run the integration tests and `make utest` to run the unit tests.
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | ## Further reading
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | To read about the benefits of using this template,
| MCP:Yl3et9 | check out [my blog post](https://bitestreams.com/blog/fastapi_template/).

| MCP:Yl3et9 | version: "3.7"
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | services:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |   api:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     depends_on:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       - db
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     ports:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000"
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     build:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       dockerfile: ./api/Dockerfile
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       context: .
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     volumes:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       - ./api:/home/user/app/api
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     environment:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       - DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@db:5432/postgres
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 |   db:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     image: postgres:14.5
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     ports:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     volumes:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
| MCP:Yl3et9 |     environment:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |       - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test
| MCP:Yl3et9 | 
| MCP:Yl3et9 | volumes:
| MCP:Yl3et9 |   postgres-data:

data.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me summarize what I found.

---

## Non-Docker Setup Path

The README under the **"Installation without Docker"** section documents the following workflow:

### Prerequisites
- **Poetry** must be installed on your machine.

### Steps
1. **Clone the repo** and `cd` into the project root.
2. **Install the Python dependencies** via Poetry:
   ```bash
   poetry install
   ```
3. **Set the `DB_STRING` environment variable** to your database connection string. For example, to use a local SQLite database file:
   ```bash
   export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
   ```
4. **Run database migrations and start the server:**
   ```bash
   poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
   ```

The app will then be available at `localhost:5000/docs`.

---

## Required Database Connection String Variable

The environment variable that **must** be set for this non-Docker workflow is:

> **`DB_STRING`**

As shown in the README example, a quick local setup uses `sqlite:///test.db/`. For comparison, the Docker Compose configuration (`docker-compose.yml`) sets the same variable to a PostgreSQL connection string:
```
DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@db:5432/postgres
```

So `DB_STRING` is the single variable the application reads for its database connection, regardless of whether you use Docker or run locally with Poetry.
